channel.title: Midnight Archive
channel.description: An English pilot edition of Midnight Archive: public-domain Chinese strange tales, source notes, literary retellings, and editorial reading guides.

item.title: Xi Fangping: The Man Who Would Not Withdraw His Complaint
item.description: A ghost story sharpened into a case file: justice does not arrive simply because the dead have entered another court.

item.title: Nie Xiaoqian: The Woman Brought Back from the Ruined Temple
item.description: A ruined-temple encounter becomes a story about restraint, trust, and a ghost who is not identical with the violence that trapped her.

item.title: Painted Skin: The Lovely Face That Hid a Hunger
item.description: A classic Liaozhai tale about beauty, warning signs, and the dangerous stories people tell themselves before they believe what they have seen.

item.title: The Taoist of Laoshan: Wanting Magic Without the Discipline
item.description: A light, sharp tale about a seeker who loves the spectacle of transcendence but not the labor that might make him worthy of it.

item.title: Wang Liulang: A Ghost at the River Who Refused a Victim
item.description: A gentle strange tale in which the expected water-ghost horror is turned toward friendship, restraint, and the refusal to pass suffering on.
